original_hypothesis.title: taxCut=0 Causes Division-by-Zero DoS Across All Economic Functions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: NukeFund.receive(), EntityForging.forgeWithListed(), and EntityTrading.buyNFT() all use taxCut as a divisor (e.g., `devShare = msg.value / taxCut`). The setTaxCut() functions in all three contracts have no validation that _taxCut > 0. If any owner sets taxCut to 0, all transactions that send ETH through that contract will revert with a division-by-zero error. For NukeFund, this blocks all minting (since _distributeFunds sends ETH to NukeFund), all trading, and all forging — a complete protocol halt.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Three independent setTaxCut functions with no minimum value validation; taxCut used as divisor in critical ETH distribution paths.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 devShare = msg.value / taxCut; — no guard against taxCut == 0; setTaxCut has no require(_taxCut > 0)

preconditions: Owner calls setTaxCut(0) on NukeFund, EntityForging, or EntityTrading

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: No input validation on setTaxCut; value directly used as divisor

operational_questions: Is there governance process or multisig preventing accidental setTaxCut(0)?
Would setting taxCut to 1 also cause issues (100% to dev, 0% to fund)?
validation_requirements: Call setTaxCut(0) then attempt mint/trade/forge — verify division by zero revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All subsequent ETH sent to NukeFund reverts (division by zero in receive())
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All minting halts (_distributeFunds sends to NukeFund which reverts)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All trading and forging also halt if their taxCut is set to 0
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Owner key compromise or malicious admin

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: No minting, trading, forging, or nuke fund deposits possible
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Owner can call setTaxCut(10) to restore; requires owner action

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
